rule.name: Authentication
rule.rule_desc: Detects file paths related to authentication functionality.
rule.vuln_desc: If this rule matches, it indicates the presence of authentication-related code files in the project.
rule.developer: Review and document authentication-related code, ensuring they are correctly implemented.
rule.reviewer: Verify the presence and correctness of authentication-related code. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Authorization
rule.rule_desc: Detects file paths related to authorization functionality.
rule.vuln_desc: If this rule matches, it indicates the presence of code paths related to authorization in the project.
rule.developer: Review and document authorization-related code, ensuring they are correctly implemented.
rule.reviewer: Verify the presence and secure implementation of authorization-related code. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Session Management
rule.rule_desc: Detects file paths related to session management functionality.
rule.vuln_desc: If this rule matches, it indicates the presence of code paths related to session management in the project.
rule.developer: Review and document session management-related code, ensuring they are correctly implemented.
rule.reviewer: Verify the presence and correctness of session management-related code. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Permission Management or Handling
rule.rule_desc: Detects file paths related to permission management or handling functionality.
rule.vuln_desc: If this rule matches, it indicates the presence of permission management or handling-related code paths in the project.
rule.developer: Review and document permission management or handling-related code paths, ensuring they are correctly implemented.
rule.reviewer: Verify the presence and correctness of permission management or handling-related code paths. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Multi-Factor Validation
rule.rule_desc: Detects file paths that may indicate the implementation or handling of Multi-Factor Validation.
rule.vuln_desc: If this rule matches, it indicates the presence of code paths related to Multi-Factor Validation in the project.
rule.developer: Thoroughly review and document code paths related to Multi-Factor Validation, ensuring they are correctly implemented.
rule.reviewer: Verify the presence and secure implementation related to Multi-Factor Validation. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Admin Section
rule.rule_desc: Detects file paths related to admin section functionality.
rule.vuln_desc: If this rule matches, it indicates the presence of admin section-related code paths in the project.
rule.developer: Review and document admin section-related code paths, ensuring they are correctly implemented.
rule.reviewer: Verify the presence and correctness of admin section-related code paths. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.vuln_desc: If this rule matches, it indicates the presence of user section-related code paths in the project.

rule.reviewer: Verify the presence and correctness of user section-related code paths. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.vuln_desc: If this rule matches, it indicates the presence of password-related code paths in the project.
rule.developer: Review and document password-related code paths, ensuring they are correctly implemented.
rule.reviewer: Verify the presence and correctness of password-related code paths. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.reviewer: Verify the presence and correctness of code paths related to sensitive data handling. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.rule_desc: Detects file paths related to security functionality.
rule.vuln_desc: If this rule matches, it indicates the presence of security-related code paths in the project.
rule.developer: Review and document security-related code paths, ensuring they are correctly implemented.
rule.reviewer: Verify the presence and correctness of security-related code paths. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Input Validation
rule.rule_desc: Detects file paths related to input validation functionality.
rule.vuln_desc: If this rule matches, it indicates the presence of code paths related to input validation in the project.
rule.developer: Review and document input validation-related code, ensuring they are correctly implemented.
rule.reviewer: Verify the presence and correctness of input validation-related code. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: API
rule.rule_desc: Detects file paths related to API functionality.
rule.vuln_desc: If this rule matches, it indicates the presence of API-related code paths in the project.
rule.developer: Review and document API-related code paths, ensuring they are correctly implemented.
rule.reviewer: Verify the presence and correctness of API-related code paths. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Libraries | Extensions | Plugins
rule.rule_desc: Detects file paths related to libraries, extensions, or plugins.
rule.vuln_desc: If this rule matches, it indicates the presence of code paths related to libraries, extensions, or plugins in the project.
rule.developer: Review and document code paths related to libraries, extensions, or plugins, ensuring they are correctly implemented.
rule.reviewer: Verify the presence and correctness of code paths related to libraries, extensions, or plugins. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Monkey Patched Code Files
rule.rule_desc: Detects the presence of monkey patched code in a project.
rule.vuln_desc: If this rule matches, it indicates the presence of monkey patched code in the project. While monkey patches themselves may not inherently contain security issues, they introduce potential maintenance challenges and risks. Ensure that these patches are well-documented, periodically reviewed for necessity, and that they do not introduce security vulnerabilities or regressions.
rule.developer: Carefully review and document any monkey patched code in the project. Ensure that the patches are correctly applied and do not introduce compatibility issues or security vulnerabilities. Consider alternatives to monkey patching when possible to maintain code stability and security.
rule.reviewer: Verify the presence of monkey patched code in the project and assess its impact on code stability and security. Particular attention should be paid to patches affecting security-critical components like authentication, authorization, or database queries. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: CAPTCHA
rule.rule_desc: Detects file paths related to CAPTCHA functionality.
rule.vuln_desc: If this rule matches, it indicates the presence of code paths related to CAPTCHA functionality in the project.
rule.developer: Review and document code paths related to CAPTCHA functionality, ensuring they are correctly implemented.
rule.reviewer: Verify the presence and correctness of code paths related to CAPTCHA functionality. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.
